ground_truth: Ten years after the Paris Agreement was signed in Le Bourget, France, global CO₂ emissions have reached record levels and temperatures are at 1.45°C above pre-industrial baseline. While the EU has cut emissions by 32%, major emitters like Brazil (deforestation) and India (2070 net zero target) are falling short. The upcoming COP30 summit in Belém, Brazil, is cited as a critical test of international resolve.
summary: According to the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC), global temperatures in 2024 were 1.45°C above the 1850–1900 baseline. The European Union has cut emissions by 32% since 1990, while Brazil faces criticism for high deforestation rates in the Amazon Basin. Shell's CEO Wael Sawan rejected a report by InfluenceMap that found major oil and gas companies spent $750 million on climate legislation lobbying between 2020 and 2024. The upcoming COP30 summit in Belém, Brazil, will test international resolve to meet the Paris Agreement goals.
